title: Neon Genesis more than just anime
display_title: Neon Genesis more than just anime
wikitext:
[]: Neon Genesis.. more than just "anime"             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:40 am Does anybody else here think of Neon Genesis Evangelion as more than just another anime series. To me, Especially episodes 21-25 and the End of Evangelion, it's more like a piece of art than just another show. The characters are animated, but they are more human than most real people you will ever see in any live action movies. Plus the visuals of Rei/Lillith standing above the earth with all her wings, while all the glowing crosses line the planet is one of the most beautiful things i've ever seen. To me Eva is something you can watch even if you don't like "anime" in general. I do like anime but Eva feels like... something more. Does anyone agree??

[]:             Quote Post by Heidroch » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:49 am I can see where you're coming from. When I first saw Eva, I was blown away for all the reasons you mentioned. It has drawn in for a couple years by now, something that no other TV show or movie has quite done for me. Funny, because I don't really consider myself an anime fan. Every other anime I have tried watching hasn't drawn me in or interested me, except for the exception of FLCL(cheers to your avatar, by the way). But even then, my interest in FLCL doesn't quite match that of Eva. It's just Eva's content that keeps me coming back for more. Hell, on the 22nd I'm about to add more to my Eva investment with the purchase of the Platinum Thinpack.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:49 am I know Evangelion is, in all simplicity, an "anime" but that's like calling Romeo and Juliet a "short story." Evangelion is one of the extremely few cases of a work becoming more than what it is. The Beatles took music to another level, Dali and Matisse took some paint and made a serious impression, I feel like Evangelion goes along those lines. You don't have to be a serious rocker to dig the Beatles, you don't have to be an art critic to like the surrealist works of Dali or Matisse, and you most certainly don't have to be a fan of anime to love Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:04 am I like how you referenced movies. I was, for whatever reason, expecting you to say "More realistic than any person." Meh, just like I see the word "Square" for no real reason. I'm an anime enthusiast. I mean, I have seen my fair share of animes (generally mainstream since I don't go out looking for anime) such as DBZ, Tenchi Muyo, Cowboy Bebop and Trigun... Plus more. I learned to like many of them and even started to get into some of them on a deeper level. But Evangelion is the ONLY anime I obsess with. I don't consider it "more than an anime" just because that's what it is. But I consider it the greatest anime ever created. It'd be interesting to see the difference between anime Pre-Evangelion and Post-Evangelion, just to see how far stretching its influence was. It was the most visually appealing, had the greatest story line (it may even have the greatest story I have ever bourn witness to), had the greatest characters (Asuka is my favorite character ever created) and had some of the best music from any anime I have seen. Of course, I am speaking from a very closed world... While I have seen a number of different animes, beginning to end, I still haven't seen even a fraction of what many "true" anime fans have seen. But, in my own closed world, Evangelion is the greatest anime ever... And it will be quite the task to try and take it from my first place position.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Nov 18, 2005 12:33 pm No I disagree strongly. NGE is a very good very though pervoking series, but its nothing more then that. Obessing over it is to miss the point and chose a fantisy world over reality.

[]:             Quote Post by fadingreminder » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:03 pm DatDude wrote: Obessing over it is to miss the point and chose a fantisy world over reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Nov 18, 2005 1:08 pm You'll tire of it eventually. You'll still like it, but it won't be sitting at the front of your mind like the fat sow it is right now.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:17 pm @Hexon Seconded. NGE does have that je ne sais quoi about it that sets itself apart from anything else I've watched. I think if you've the right sort of personality it will sink its hooks in, and it takes time to make an accomodation with it.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:42 pm Thanks for all your opinions!! I agree that you don't have to like anime to like NGE. It was the first anime I liked and I didn't think I was even going to buy anymore since I was so obsessed, but since it's been over a year I have bought many other anime. FLCL is pretty close to Neon Genesis in awesomeness factor. and yeah Evangelion's story is my favourite one ever too. I don't think one day has gone by since that day a year ago that I haven't thought about Evangelion at least once a day.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Fri Nov 18, 2005 2:57 pm Of cousre evangelion is more than an anime to me. With out it I wouldnt have met Kaworu, Rei, Shinji, or Misato. Love always Yuko-chan

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:48 pm wait... what if I think animation is artwork? All anime and cartoons are artwork. Unless we're talking about Squidbillies or somethin.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Fri Nov 18, 2005 3:50 pm yes it's true that all animation is artwork. but what I mean is Neon Genesis just took it to a whole new level with me.

[]:             Quote Post by Toilet Duck » Tue Nov 22, 2005 2:27 am i consider evangelion quite thought-provoking and in the vein of such experimental literature as the book "flowers for algernon" (another mixing of science fiction and "psychological" aspects). as an anime, i must confess that it sometimes rivals .hack//SIGN in making my brain hurt from wrapping itself around all of the details and dialogue. it's also jockeying with last exile, azumanga daioh, and r.o.d the tv for the title of my all-time favorite anime series.

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Wed Nov 23, 2005 10:36 am Neon Genesis Evangelion, is a true piece of artwork I agree. It's like the Van Gogh of Anime. The characters have true feelings, unlike many other Anime series, and it's a little more realistic, putting aside the EVAs and Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Wed Nov 23, 2005 2:04 pm superdoughboy4 wrote: putting aside the EVAs and Angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Nov 23, 2005 3:48 pm Tomato is a fruit. Evangelion is an anime.

[]:             Quote Post by rb_182_ » Thu Nov 24, 2005 3:23 am Yes it is an anime. as in "Japanese animation". but I know people who don't like anime but like Neon Genesis because it's a lot better than most anime out there. There are a lot of other series i'm starting to like as much as eva now. but man it will always have a place in my heart.. and in my brain.. damn unexplained mysteries.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Thu Nov 24, 2005 4:00 pm Phaze wrote: Tomato is a fruit. Evangelion is an anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Ion » Thu Nov 24, 2005 8:38 pm Evangelion is real art and there are few things equal to it. Every true artist makes it own language to express his ideas like. Tomato is a fruit. Evangelion is an anime.

[]:             Quote Post by geishaboy » Fri Nov 25, 2005 1:38 am I'm kinda on the fence with this one. I would be inclined to say that it is more than just an anime.
